length_bias.method: char-length of each option text with #feedback stripped; longest-correct when len(correct) > mean(distractors) * 1.20
length_bias.note: meta undercounts (claims 1/6); re-derived rate 2/6 still under threshold.

distractor_plausibility.note: every distractor is a real beginner misconception (tier mis-ordering, law-vs-guideline, cleverness-vs-use, conformity-check scope confusion).

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: EU AI Act spelled out in q1. Tests risk-tier structure only. No NIST / US-EO / India trivia, no article numbers, no penalty amounts. q6's four-pillars reference is to earlier topic 5.4 (corpus §5.7, LO5) — in-scope, not adjacent trivia.

verdict: Pass — positional bias 0.333, length bias 0.333 (re-derived 2/6, meta undercounts 1/6 but still under threshold), distractors all plausible misconceptions, all 5 LOs covered, beginner-fair and on-headline, GIFT clean. No meta/gift desync on position distribution.
